summary: Read{"file_path": "/home/user/app/products.json"}### products.json contents: ```json {   "products": [     {       "id": 1,       "name": "Widget A",       "price": 10.99     },     {       "id": 2,       "name": "Widget B",       "price": 20.99     },     {       "id": 3,       "name": "Widget C",       "price": 30.99     },     {       "id": 4,       "name": "Widget D",       "price": 40.99     },     {       "id": 5,       "name": "Widget E",       "price": 50.99     }   ] } ```The problem is that the product list cache is not being updated after creating a new product. This is causing the test to fail because the new product is not visible in the list. To fix this, we need to update the product list cache after creating a new product. This can be done by adding a function to update the cache in the create_product function. The cache should be updated by reading the products.json file and storing the contents in the cache variable. This will ensure that the cache is always up-to-date and the new product is visible in the list. The fix is to add the following code to the create_product function: ```python # Update the cache with the new product list cache = json.load(open('products.json')) ``` This will update the cache with the new product list and ensure that the test passes.
